# Sample slurm.conf[¶](#sample-slurmconf "Permanent link")

This page provides a sample `slurm.conf` with inline comments explaining each parameter. Omnia auto-generates a `slurm.conf` if none is provided; use this reference to create a custom configuration when needed.

## Complete sample[¶](#complete-sample "Permanent link")

File: /etc/slurm/slurm.conf
 
 
 # ===========================================================================
 # slurm.conf -- Slurm configuration file
 # Generated by Omnia or customized by the administrator.
 # Place this file at the path specified by omnia_config.yml:slurm_config_path
 # or let Omnia auto-generate it.
 # ===========================================================================
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Cluster identification
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 ClusterName=omnia_cluster # Unique name for this Slurm cluster.
 SlurmctldHost=slurm-ctrl-01 # Hostname of the Slurm controller node.
 # Must match the hostname in the PXE
 # mapping file.
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Daemon paths and PID files
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 SlurmctldPidFile=/var/run/slurmctld.pid
 SlurmdPidFile=/var/run/slurmd.pid
 SlurmdSpoolDir=/var/spool/slurmd
 StateSaveLocation=/var/spool/slurmctld
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Authentication
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 AuthType=auth/munge # Use Munge for inter-daemon authentication.
 CryptoType=crypto/munge # Munge-based message signing.
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Logging
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 SlurmctldLogFile=/var/log/slurmctld.log
 SlurmdLogFile=/var/log/slurmd.log
 SlurmctldDebug=info # Log level: quiet, fatal, error, info,
 # verbose, debug, debug2-debug5.
 SlurmdDebug=info
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Scheduling
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 SchedulerType=sched/backfill # Backfill scheduler for improved utilization.
 SelectType=select/cons_tres # Track consumable resources (CPUs, memory,
 # GPUs) per node.
 SelectTypeParameters=CR_Core_Memory # Schedule by core and memory.
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Job accounting
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 AccountingStorageType=accounting_storage/slurmdbd
 AccountingStorageHost=slurm-ctrl-01 # Host running slurmdbd.
 AccountingStoragePort=6819 # Port for slurmdbd connections.
 JobAcctGatherType=jobacct_gather/linux # Collect job resource usage from
 # the Linux kernel.
 JobAcctGatherFrequency=30 # Gather interval in seconds.
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Process tracking
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 ProctrackType=proctrack/cgroup # Use cgroups to track job processes.
 TaskPlugin=task/cgroup,task/affinity # cgroup-based resource isolation
 # and CPU affinity.
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Timers and limits
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 SlurmctldTimeout=120 # Seconds before slurmctld is considered
 # unresponsive.
 SlurmdTimeout=300 # Seconds before a node is marked DOWN.
 InactiveLimit=0 # No limit on idle interactive jobs.
 MinJobAge=300 # Minimum seconds before a completed job
 # record is purged from memory.
 MaxJobCount=100000 # Maximum number of jobs in the queue.
 KillWait=30 # Seconds between SIGTERM and SIGKILL
 # when canceling a job.
 WaitTime=0 # Seconds to wait after first task
 # allocation before starting the job.
 ReturnToService=2 # Automatically return DOWN nodes to
 # service when they report back.
 # 2 = return even if addr/hostname changed.
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # GPU (GRES) configuration
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 GresTypes=gpu # Enable GPU as a Generic Resource.
 # Per-node GRES definitions are in gres.conf or auto-detected.
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Partitions (queues)
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Default partition -- all CPU compute nodes.
 PartitionName=compute Nodes=slurm-cpu-[01-10] Default=YES MaxTime=INFINITE State=UP
 
 # GPU partition -- nodes equipped with GPUs.
 PartitionName=gpu Nodes=slurm-gpu-[01-04] MaxTime=INFINITE State=UP
 
 # Debug partition -- short, high-priority jobs for testing.
 PartitionName=debug Nodes=slurm-cpu-[01-02] MaxTime=01:00:00 State=UP Priority=100
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Node definitions
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # CPU-only nodes
 NodeName=slurm-cpu-[01-10] CPUs=64 RealMemory=256000 Sockets=2 CoresPerSocket=32 ThreadsPerCore=1 State=UNKNOWN
 
 # GPU nodes (4x NVIDIA A100 per node)
 NodeName=slurm-gpu-[01-04] CPUs=128 RealMemory=512000 Sockets=2 CoresPerSocket=64 ThreadsPerCore=1 Gres=gpu:nvidia_a100:4 State=UNKNOWN
 

## Key parameter reference[¶](#key-parameter-reference "Permanent link")

Parameter | Description 
---|--- 
`ClusterName` | Unique identifier for the Slurm cluster. Used in job accounting. 
`SlurmctldHost` | Hostname of the controller node; must be resolvable by all nodes. 
`SchedulerType` | `sched/backfill` is recommended for production; fills gaps with smaller jobs. 
`SelectType` | `select/cons_tres` enables fine-grained tracking of CPUs, memory, and GPUs. 
`GresTypes` | Comma-separated list of generic resource types (e.g., `gpu`). 
`PartitionName` | Defines a job queue with a set of nodes, time limit, and state. 
`NodeName` | Declares a node or node range with hardware specifications. 
 
Note

 * Adjust `CPUs`, `RealMemory`, `Sockets`, `CoresPerSocket`, and `Gres` to match your actual hardware. Mismatched values cause nodes to register as `DRAIN`.
 * Node names must match the hostnames in the PXE mapping file.

Info
